cards.title: Jack's Wife Freda
cards.address: 226 Lafayette St, New York, NY 10012, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
A warm, all-day SoHo staple that sets the right tone for the first full morning — relaxed, unpretentious, genuinely good food.

**Know before you go**
- The shakshuka and green eggs are the signature dishes — order either and you won't regret it
- Gets busy fast on weekends; arrive right at 8:30 to beat the brunch crowd
- Kid-friendly without being a kids' restaurant — the vibe is neighbourhood, not tourist
- Lafayette St location is a 5-minute walk from 11 Howard — easy start to the day

cards.title: Museum of Ice Cream
cards.address: 558 Broadway, New York, NY 10012, USA

details.notes:
Arrive 10 minutes early. Bring mobile ticket and photo ID (one per group). Complete Pre-Entry Waiver before arrival. Show mobile ticket at check-in.

Arrive 10 minutes early. Bring mobile ticket and photo ID (one per group). Complete Pre-Entry Waiver before arrival. Show mobile ticket at check-in.
details.included: Unlimited ice cream tastings, immersive installations, slides, Sprinkle Pool, creative activities, seasonal cocktails

cards.title: Pearl & The Beast
cards.address: 22 Howard St basement, New York, NY 10013, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
A hidden, immersive underground experience that feels like stumbling into a secret — genuinely unlike anything else on the itinerary.

**Know before you go**
- Entrance is through a basement door on Howard St — easy to walk past, look for the signage carefully
- The experience is interactive and story-driven; Mia will be pulled into the narrative from the start
- No phones during the experience — part of what makes it special
- Allow buffer time before and after; it's in the same block as 11 Howard which makes logistics easy
- Check the booking for any age restrictions or time slot specifics before the day

cards.title: Parm Mulberry Street
cards.address: 248 Mulberry St, New York, NY 10012, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
A no-nonsense, red-sauce Italian-American classic in the heart of NoLita — the right energy for a relaxed family dinner.

**Know before you go**
- The chicken parm is the dish; order it, there's no debate
- Portions are large — one main between two is fine for a kid
- NoLita street outside is charming for a post-dinner walk down to Spring St
- Reservations recommended for 6 PM on a weekend; walk-ins possible but risky in summer
- Casual and loud in the best way — no pressure to be on your best behaviour

cards.title: Wicked
cards.address: 222 W 51st St, New York, NY 10019, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
Mia's first Broadway show — and one of the best possible introductions to live theatre for a kid who already knows every song.

**Know before you go**
- Arrive at the theatre by 6:30 PM — Broadway seats fill fast and the pre-show energy is part of the experience
- She will know the music; let her sing along quietly, nobody minds at Aladdin
- The staging and costumes are genuinely spectacular — the magic carpet scene alone is worth the ticket price
- Book the best seats you can afford in the orchestra or front mezzanine; the sightlines matter for a 7-year-old
- There's an intermission — use it to get her a snack and let the first act land before talking about it

cards.title: Mimi's Frozen Yogurt
cards.address: 231 Lafayette St, New York, NY 10012, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
A saved discovery option — small fro-yo shop, good for a hot-afternoon Mia reset between activities.

**Know before you go**
- Soft-serve style, customize your toppings — perfect kid-friendly drop-in
- Decide on the day based on what neighbourhood you're in; not worth routing a day around
- Good summer-afternoon palate cleanser if the heat is high and she needs a pause

cards.title: Sadelle's New York
cards.address: 463 W Broadway, New York, NY 10012, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
A polished SoHo breakfast institution — optional this morning if you're sleeping in after Aladdin the night before. If you're up, it's worth it; if not, head straight to AMNH.

**Know before you go**
- The tower of bagels with all the accompaniments is the signature order — worth doing at least once
- Expect a wait on weekends; put your name in and walk the block while you wait
- More of a sit-down, slower experience than a grab-and-go — plan accordingly
- Located on W Broadway, a 3-minute walk from 11 Howard

cards.title: Black Seed Bagels | Nolita
cards.address: 170 Elizabeth St, New York, NY 10012, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
A Montreal-style bagel shop in NoLita — a practical breakfast backup if Sadelle's has an impossible wait or you want something quicker.

**Know before you go**
- Bagels are baked in a wood-fired oven, Montreal-style — smaller, denser, slightly sweet compared to NY style
- Classic order: everything bagel with smoked salmon cream cheese
- 6-minute walk from 11 Howard — easy grab-and-go option
- Faster than Sadelle's; the line moves quickly and you can take it to go
- Open from early morning; fine as a pre-activity pit stop

cards.title: Leon's Bagels
cards.address: 169 Thompson St suite b, New York, NY 10012, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
A saved bagel option from discovery — unscheduled. Likely a backup or an alternative to Black Seed / Sadelle's depending on where you are that morning.

**Know before you go**
- Decide on the day based on location; not worth detouring for
- Good traditional NY-style bagels; straightforward, no-frills shop
- Cut this card if you stick with Sadelle's and Black Seed as your two bagel plays — three is more than the trip needs

cards.title: Gershwin Theatre
cards.address: 222 W 51st St, New York, NY 10019, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
A duplicate Gershwin Theatre card — the real Wicked booking is on Day 2 (July 24) at 7:00 PM with the full note. Worth deleting post-trip.

**Know before you go**
- See the Day 2 Wicked card for arrival time, seating advice, and pre-show guidance

cards.title: Pearson Airport Terminal 1 Parking
cards.address: Pearson Airport Terminal 1 Parking, Mississauga, ON L5P, Canada

details.notes:
**Intent**
A duplicate Pearson Parking card from import — the real scheduled flight logistics is the 9:20 AM card above. Worth deleting post-trip.

**Know before you go**
- See the 9:20 AM card for flight number, terminal, and timing details

cards.title: Rubirosa
cards.address: 235 Mulberry St, New York, NY 10012, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
A duplicate Rubirosa card — the real 6:00 PM dinner is above with the full note. Worth deleting post-trip.

**Know before you go**
- See the 6:00 PM Rubirosa card for the vodka pizza, the timing, and the no-reservations strategy

cards.title: Rockefeller Center
cards.address: 45 Rockefeller Plaza, New York, NY 10111, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
Saved from discovery, unscheduled. Decide on the day based on energy and weather.

**Know before you go**
- The Top of the Rock observation deck is the genuine attraction; book tickets in advance if you want to go up — sunset slots go first
- At street level, Rockefeller Plaza is worth a 15-minute walk-through even without going up — Mia will recognize the ice rink spot from movies
- Close to Broadway theatres; logical to pair with pre-Wicked time on Day 2 if you have a buffer
- Skip entirely if the day is already full — this is a nice-to-have, not a must-do for a 4-day trip

cards.title: 11 Howard
cards.address: 11 Howard St, New York, NY 10013, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
A duplicate 11 Howard card — the real booking is the scheduled 12 PM check-in above. Kept for now so the map pin and reference data stay intact. Worth deleting post-trip.

**Know before you go**
- See the 12:00 PM 11 Howard card for confirmation number, check-in time, and hotel notes

cards.title: Museum of Ice Cream
cards.address: 558 Broadway, New York, NY 10012, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
A duplicate Museum of Ice Cream card — the real 10:30 AM booking is on Day 2 with tickets confirmed. Worth deleting post-trip.

**Know before you go**
- See the Day 2 10:30 AM Museum of Ice Cream card for ticket details and arrival guidance

cards.title: Pearson Airport Terminal 1 Parking
cards.address: Pearson Airport Terminal 1 Parking, Mississauga, ON L5P, Canada

cards.title: 11 Howard
cards.address: 11 Howard St, New York, NY 10013, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
A design-forward SoHo hotel that doubles as a perfect base — the location puts everything on the itinerary within walking distance.

**Know before you go**
- Minimum check-in age is 21; bring valid ID
- Late arrivals after 1 AM require advance notice to the property
- Howard St is one of the quieter streets in SoHo — the neighbourhood feels calm despite being central
- Crosby Street and the SoHo cast-iron district are literally outside the door — great for an evening walk with Mia
- Confirm check-in time in advance; the 12 PM slot is likely early check-in, not guaranteed
details.address: 11 Howard Street, New York, NY, 10013, United States of America

cards.title: Sloomoo Institute
cards.address: 475 Broadway, New York, NY 10013, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
Pure sensory chaos in the best possible way — a slime experience that was basically engineered for a 7-year-old's birthday year.

**Know before you go**
- Book tickets well in advance; this is a popular summer activity and slots fill fast
- Wear old clothes or bring a change — slime gets on everything
- The experience runs about 75–90 minutes; kids tend to lose track of time completely
- Located on Broadway, a 5-minute walk from 11 Howard — perfectly placed after hotel check-in
- They sell slime to take home at the end; budget for it because she will want some

Signed waivers required for all party members before visit - anyone without a signed waiver will be denied entry. You may arrive within 30 minutes after your ticket entry time. If you arrive later, you may need to wait or may be denied entry. Tickets should be presented upon arrival. Recommend wearing pants and shirts with sleeves you can easily roll up - you will likely get slimey. Slime can be removed from clothing using vinegar and water. Recommend wearing long hair up. Nearest trains: NQRW at Canal St, 6 at Spring St, ACE at Canal St, BDFM at Broadway Lafayette.
details.address: 475 Broadway, between Broome and Grand Streets, New York, NY
includes: All tickets are non-refundable. Rescheduling subject to availability up to 2 hours in advance.
details.cancellation_policy: All tickets are non-refundable. Rescheduling subject to availability up to 2 hours in advance.

cards.title: Rubirosa
cards.address: 235 Mulberry St, New York, NY 10012, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
The best vodka pizza in New York, full stop — a NoLita institution that earns its cult status every single night.

**Know before you go**
- Order the vodka sauce pizza; everything else is secondary
- No reservations — arrive at 6 PM on the dot or expect a wait
- The room is small and energetic; not a quiet dinner but a great one
- It's 2 minutes from 11 Howard — possibly the most convenient great restaurant on the whole itinerary
- The garlic knots are mandatory

cards.title: Morgenstern's Finest Ice Cream
cards.address: 2 Rivington St, New York, NY 10002, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
A post-dinner ritual disguised as an ice cream stop — one of the best in the city and the perfect way to end a big day.

**Know before you go**
- The burnt honey vanilla and black coconut ash are the signature flavours — start there
- Small shop; expect a short queue on summer evenings but it moves fast
- On Rivington St in the Lower East Side — a 10-minute walk or quick Uber from NoLita after dinner
- One scoop each is the move; the flavours are rich and you'll want to try more than one

cards.title: Sadelle's New York
cards.address: 463 W Broadway, New York, NY 10012, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
A polished SoHo breakfast institution — optional this morning if you're sleeping in after Aladdin the night before. If you're up, it's worth it; if not, head straight to AMNH.

**Know before you go**
- The tower of bagels with all the accompaniments is the signature order — worth doing at least once
- Expect a wait on weekends; put your name in and walk the block while you wait
- More of a sit-down, slower experience than a grab-and-go — plan accordingly
- Located on W Broadway, a 3-minute walk from 11 Howard

cards.title: Museum of Natural History + Gilder Center
cards.address: 200 Central Prk W, New York, NY 10024, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
A full morning purpose-built for a space-obsessed 7-year-old — the Rose Center alone is worth the trip, and the dinosaur halls are the best bonus in the world.

**Know before you go**
- Start at the Rose Center for Earth and Space the moment doors open — the Hayden Planetarium Space Show is the headline experience and sells out; book it in advance separately from museum tickets
- The Hall of the Universe inside the Rose Center breaks down galaxies, dark matter, and the cosmic microwave background in a way that will genuinely fascinate her
- After the Space Show, cross to the fourth floor dinosaur halls — still incredible and a natural second act
- The Hall of Ocean Life (giant blue whale) is a good wind-down before lunch — dramatic and calming at the same time
- Allow 3.5–4 hours minimum; Space Center + dinosaurs + ocean life is a perfect full morning

cards.title: Levain Bakery
cards.address: 167 Orchard St, New York, NY 10002, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
A 10-minute detour that becomes one of the most talked-about moments of the trip — New York's most legendary cookie, warm, on the walk to lunch.

**Know before you go**
- Order the chocolate chip walnut or dark chocolate chocolate chip — both are enormous and meant to be shared
- It's a 2-minute walk off the Columbus Ave route between AMNH and Shake Shack, no backtracking
- Expect a short queue; it moves fast
- One cookie between two is genuinely enough — they're the size of a small fist and dense
- Eat it walking; that's the correct way

cards.title: Shake Shack Upper West Side
cards.address: 366 Columbus Ave, New York, NY 10024, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
A practical, universally loved lunch stop right by the museum — fast, good, and requires zero decision-making after a big morning.

**Know before you go**
- The Columbus Ave location is a 3-minute walk from the museum exit
- Order the ShackBurger and crinkle fries; the vanilla milkshake is non-negotiable for Mia
- Can get busy at peak lunch hour — order ahead on the app to skip the line
- Eat outside if the weather cooperates; the Upper West Side streets are pleasant in July

cards.title: Milk Bar
cards.address: 1196 Broadway, New York, NY 10001

details.notes:
**Intent**
A hands-on cake decorating class at Milk Bar — Mia makes something real with the team behind Cereal Milk, Milk Bar Pie, and Christina Tosi's whole empire. This is the trip's centrepiece memory, not just an activity.

**Know before you go**
- Class runs 2–4:30 PM; arrive 10 minutes early
- Located at 1196 Broadway in NoMad (between 28th and 29th) — not the retail shop location, this is the dedicated classes studio
- From Shake Shack Upper West Side, subway or Uber takes 20–25 minutes; plan the transition with buffer
- Wear something that can handle frosting and sprinkles
- The class is guided and beginner-friendly — no prior baking experience needed
- She takes home what she makes; bring a box or bag for the trip back to the hotel
- Confirm the booking covers two participants; some classes have age minimums, worth double-checking the confirmation

cards.title: Minetta Tavern
cards.address: 113 MacDougal St, New York, NY 10012, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
A classic West Village bistro with serious pedigree — the right room for a slightly more grown-up dinner on the last night.

**Know before you go**
- The Black Label Burger is one of the best burgers in New York — order it without overthinking
- Reservations essentially required; book as far in advance as possible, especially for summer weekends
- The room is dark, warm, and theatrical — Mia will feel like she's somewhere special
- On MacDougal St in the West Village, close to the Milk Bar class location — easy to flow from one to the other
- Dress slightly smarter than casual; the vibe rewards it

cards.title: DeSalvio Playground
cards.address: 44 Spring St, New York, NY 10012, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
The perfect sugar-burn after Economy Candy — a neighbourhood playground right on the route back to the hotel, relaxed and zero pressure.

**Know before you go**
- On Spring St, a straight 10-minute walk from Economy Candy back toward SoHo
- Small and local — not a destination playground, but that's the point on a departure morning
- Grab a coffee from a nearby café and sit while she plays — you've earned it
- Keep it to 45 minutes max; you need to be packed and out of the hotel by 1 PM

cards.title: Russ & Daughters Cafe
cards.address: 127 Orchard St, New York, NY 10002, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
A proper Lower East Side institution — the right place for your last breakfast in New York. Smoked fish, bagels, and a room that feels like old Manhattan in the best way.

**Know before you go**
- The classic Benny's Board (smoked fish, cream cheese, bagel chips) is the table order — share it
- Reservations essentially required on a weekend morning; book well in advance
- Located on Orchard St in the LES — about 15 minutes by Uber or subway from 11 Howard
- Budget 45–60 minutes; this is a sit-down meal, not a grab-and-go
- After breakfast, Economy Candy is a 5-minute walk away — tight logistical flow for the morning
- Cash tip is appreciated; service is old-school attentive

cards.title: Economy Candy
cards.address: 108 Rivington St, New York, NY 10002, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
A legendary floor-to-ceiling candy shop on the Lower East Side — overwhelming in the best possible way, and the perfect last-morning memory for Mia.

**Know before you go**
- Been there since 1937 — the chaos and density of it is part of the experience, let her take it all in before grabbing a basket
- Give her a budget and let her choose everything herself — that's the whole point
- Pick up a souvenir mix to bring home; they have bulk candy you can bag yourself
- It's a small shop and gets busy, especially on Sunday mornings — go early to beat the crowd
- Rivington St is a great street to wander after; grab a coffee nearby before heading back toward SoHo

cards.title: LaGuardia Airport
cards.address: East Elmhurst, NY 11371, USA

details.notes:
**Intent**
The logistical close — keep the morning relaxed and leave the hotel by 2 PM at the latest.

**Know before you go**
- LGA is notoriously congested in summer; build in buffer time, especially on a Thursday afternoon
- Terminal B (the new one) is genuinely good — plenty of food and space to decompress before the flight
- Uber or taxi from SoHo to LGA is typically 30–45 minutes but can stretch to 60+ in traffic
- Leave nothing planned after 1 PM on departure day; the city will find a way to make you late if you do

cards.title: Brooklyn Bridge
cards.address: Brooklyn Bridge, New York, NY 10038, USA
